content_en:
Raisins arz similar to the type of grape they are made of, as sweet raisins are hot, sour raisins are cold and constipating and white raisins are more effective constipating agents. Raisin pulp is suitable for the windpipe and relieves coughing, liver, and prostate pain. It In addition strengthens the stomach and soothes the intestinal cavity.

—— The Prophetic Medicine

Sweet raisin pulps are more nutritious than grapes, but less nutritious than dried figs. In addition, raisin pulps possess maturing qualities and mildly conszipate. In general, raisins strengthen the stomach, the spleen and the liver and benefit tile throat, chest, liver and prostate.

The best way to have raisins is to eat them without the seeds. Raisins provide good nutrition arid do not cause clogs like dates do. Eating raisins with their seeds I increase the nutritious intake and will benefit the stomach, liver and spleen. When one is suffering from loose nails and places raisin pulps on them, they will! Remove the nails quickly. Sweet, seedless raisins benefit for who suffer from excessive wetness and phlegm and help the liver.

Raisins In addition help memo-y power, as AzZuhri said, "Those who seek to memorize the Hadith: should eat raisins," In addition, it was reported that Abdullah Ibn 'Abbas described the raisins that, "Their seeds are an ailment while their pulp is cure.

The Prophetic Medicine 4. Zanjabil (Ginger)

"And they will be give) to drink there of a cup (of wine) mixed with anjabtl (ginger)." (76: 7)

Abu Nu'aym In addition narrated in his book on the Prophetic medicine that Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri said:

Ginger is hot in the second degree and wet in the first degree. It heats the body, helps the ingestion process, softens the stomach mildly, helps open the clogs of the liver that are caused by coldness and wetness and helps against the wetness that causes sight impairment, when it is eaten or used as kohl. Ginger In addition strengthens the sexual power and decomposes the thick gases accumulating in the intestines and the stomach.

In general, ginger is good for the cold temperament of the liver and the stomach. When one takes some ginger along with two measures of sugar and some hot water, it will work as a laxative and will rid the body of harmful excrements. Ginger is an effective

——— The Prophetic Medicine ingredient in the ointments that dissolve the phlegm.

Sour ginger is hot and dry and it excites the sexual drive, increases semen production, heats the stomach and the liver, helps the digestion process, dries the phlczm that prevails in the body and increases the memory power. It is In addition suitable for the coldness in the liver and stomach and washes the harmful effect of eating fruit. It In addition makes the scent better and fends off the harm of thick, cold foods.

The Letter Seen 1, Senna

We mentioned Senna before and stated that it is In addition called Sanut. There are seven opinions regarding what the word Senna means.

Senna is said to mean honey, the thick juice that appears on the surface of the butter and which looks like black stripes, cumin like seeds, Persian cumin, dill, dried dates or fennel.

Quince is cold and dry, constipates and benefits the stomach. Sweet quince is cold and dry and is somewhat mild. Sour quince constipates more than sweet

quince and is colder and drier. All types of Safarjal quench the thirst, stop vomiting, help produce urine and constipate. Safarjal In addition helps against stomach ulcers, hemoptysis (spitting up blood), diarrhea and nausea. Safarjal In addition prevents the ascending of gaseous material when one eats it after the meal. In addition, the ashes of washed Safarjal leaves and stems are similar to zinc in benefit.

Consuming Safarjal before the meal constipates, while consuming it after the meals softens the stool and helps the digestion process. Eating excessive amounts of Safarjal harms the nerves and causes painful constipation. Safarjal helps extinguish the yellow bile of the stomach.

When the quince is broiled, it will become softer and milder. When one removes the pips of. the quince, stuffs them with honey, dips them in a paste and then broil them under hot ashes, they will become very beneficial.

The best way to eat the quince is broiled or cooked in honey. The seeds of Safarjal In addition help against dryness in the throat, the windpipe and several other ailments. Its oil stops sweating and strengthens the stomach. Safarjal jam In addition strengthens the stomach and the liver and relieves the heart and the soul.

—— The Prophetic Medicine

In the Sahihain it is narrated that the Prophet b said:

"If it was not for the fact that my nation would not be able to bear it, I would have ordered them to use Siwak before every prayer."

In addition, in the Sahihain it is narrated that:

"Whenever the Prophet as would wake up at night, he would clean his mouth with Siwak."

Al-Kukhari In addition narrated that she Prophet b said:

“Siwak cleans the mouth and pleases the Lord." Further, Muslim rarrated that:

"Whenever the Prophet b would enter his house, he would first use the Siwak."

There are many other Ahadith on this subject.

An authentic Hadith In addition stated that just before he died, the Prophet b used the Siwak. The Prophet b said:

I have troubled you by frequently stressing using

the Siwak." The best type of Siwak is made from Arak trees. Further, one should not use an unknown tree for Siwak for it might be poisonous. In addition, Siwak should be used moderately, because it might cause the coating of the teeth to wear away and thus the teeth will be susceptible to the hot vapors or gasses that ascend from the stomach and to all kinds of dirt. When the Siwak is used moderately, it will polish the teeth, strengthen the roots of the teeth, help the tongue, prevent plaque, perfume the breath, clear the mind and strengthen the appetite.

The best way to use the Swak is by soaking it in rose water. In addition, the best type of Siwak is made from walnut stems. The author of At-Taysir said, "It was said that when one uses the Siwak once every five days, it will clear the head, sharpen the senses and alert the mind.”

Siwak is beneficial for many reasons, such as perfuming the breath, strengthening the gingiva, clearing the phlegm and the sight and preventing cavities. It In addition preserves the health of the stomach, clears the voice, helps the digestion process, helps make the speech clear and encourages one to recite the Qura'an, remember Allah and pray. Siwak In addition fights sleepiness, pleases the Lord, delights the angels and increases the number of the good deeds.

——— The Prophetic Medicine

Siwak is preferred at any time, especially before the prayer, ablution, when waking up and when the mouth emits foul odor. It is In addition preferred for those who are fasting and all other people at all times. There are Ahadith that allow this practice for those who are fasting, because it is beneficial for them and pleases the Lord. Pleasing the Lord when one is fasting is even more desired than at other times. In addition the Siwak cleans the mouth, and cleanliness for the fasting person is one of his best deeds.

In the Sunan it is narrated that ‘Amir bin RabT'ah said: "I saw the Messenger of Allah b on nvmerous occasion using the Siwak when he was fasting."

AlI-Bukhari In addition said that Ibn 'Umar said that the Prophet sg used the Siwak at the two ends of the day.

There is a consensus that the fasting person rinses his mouth while performing ablution, and rinsing the mouth is an obligation or a more preferred act than using the Siwak. In addition, Allah does not have a use for the people to worship him while their mouths are emitting foul odor, nor is it an act of worship. When the Prophet mentioned that the odor that the mouth of the fasting person emits is better in His Sight than the scent of Musk, he meant to encourage the practice of fasting, not the

practice of allowing the mouth to emit foul odor. Hence, the fasting person needs the Siwak more than anyone else. In addition, Allah's Pleasure is much better than His preferring the odor that the mouth of the fasting person emits.

Further, Allah prefers using the Siwak more than for the odor that comes out of the mouth of the fasting person to remain.

In addition, using Siwak does not prevent the good scent that Allah creates in the fasting person's mouth on the Day of Resurrection. Rather, the mouth of the fasting person will smell better than Musk on the Day of Resurrection as a sign of his observing the Fast, even if the Siwak removed the odor that used to come from his mouth. Similarly, whoever suffers a wound [for the sake of Allah] in this life is commanded to remove the blood from his body in this life, but his wound would still have the color of blood on the Day of Resurrection.

Furthermore, the Siwak does not completely remove the odor of the mouth for the fasting person because its reason still remains, that is, the stomach being empty of food. Only the effect of this fact will be lessened from the teeth and gums.

The Prophet b taught his nation the preferred and the disliked acts of the Fast. He did not mention the Siwak

—— The Prophetic Medicine

among the disliked acts although he knew they were using it. Rather, he encouraged them in general to use the Siwak and they saw him using it himself at innumerable times, knowing that they would imitate him. Never did he discourage them from using the Siwak in the afternoon (when they are fasting). It is a fact that the Prophet “g is required to offer the religious ruling when it is needed.

There is a weak Hadith that narrates that the Prophet as encouraged the Muslim to drink cow milk, because it is a remedy and because the butter of that milk is a cure, while stating that cow meat is an ailment, The chain of narrators for this Hadith is not authentic.

Somn is hot and wet in the first degree, it is mild, and helps eliminate the various tumors that attack the soft parts of the body. Somn is stronger than butter as a maturing agent, Galinus said that he used shortening to cure the tumors that occur in the ear and the tip of the nose. In addition, when the base of the teeth is anointed with shortening, the teeth will emerge faster.
content_id:
Kismis serupa dengan jenis anggur yang digunakan untuk membuatnya; kismis manis bersifat panas, kismis asam bersifat dingin dan menyebabkan sembelit, sedangkan kismis putih lebih efektif sebagai agen penyebab sembelit. Daging buah kismis cocok untuk saluran napas dan meredakan batuk, nyeri hati, serta nyeri prostat. Selain itu, ia juga menguatkan lambung dan menenangkan rongga usus.

—— Thibbun Nabawi

Daging buah kismis manis lebih bergizi daripada anggur, namun kurang bergizi dibandingkan buah ara kering. Selain itu, daging buah kismis memiliki sifat mematangkan dan sedikit menyebabkan sembelit. Secara umum, kismis menguatkan lambung, limpa, dan hati serta bermanfaat bagi tenggorokan, dada, hati, dan prostat.

Cara terbaik mengonsumsi kismis adalah memakannya tanpa biji. Kismis memberikan nutrisi yang baik dan tidak menyebabkan sumbatan seperti kurma. Memakan kismis beserta bijinya meningkatkan asupan nutrisi dan bermanfaat bagi lambung, hati, dan limpa. Apabila seseorang menderita kuku yang longgar dan meletakkan daging buah kismis di atasnya, kuku tersebut akan terlepas dengan cepat. Kismis manis tanpa biji bermanfaat bagi mereka yang menderita kelebihan kelembapan dan dahak serta membantu fungsi hati.

Kismis juga membantu daya ingat, sebagaimana Az-Zuhri berkata, "Barangsiapa yang ingin menghafal hadith hendaklah memakan kismis." Selain itu, dilaporkan bahwa Abdullah Ibn 'Abbas menggambarkan kismis dengan mengatakan, "Bijinya adalah penyakit sedangkan dagingnya adalah obat."

Thibbun Nabawi 4. Zanjabil (Jahe)

"Dan mereka akan diberi minum di sana secangkir (minuman) yang dicampur dengan zanjabil (jahe)." (76:17)

Abu Nu'aym juga meriwayatkan dalam kitabnya tentang pengobatan Nabawi bahwa Abu Sa'id Al-Khudri berkata:

Jahe bersifat panas pada tingkat kedua dan lembap pada tingkat pertama. Ia memanaskan tubuh, membantu proses pencernaan, melembutkan lambung secara ringan, membantu membuka sumbatan hati yang disebabkan oleh dingin dan kelembapan, serta membantu mengatasi kelembapan yang menyebabkan gangguan penglihatan, baik ketika dimakan maupun digunakan sebagai celak. Jahe juga menguatkan kemampuan seksual dan menguraikan gas-gas kental yang menumpuk di usus dan lambung.

Secara umum, jahe baik untuk watak dingin hati dan lambung. Apabila seseorang mengonsumsi jahe bersama dua takaran gula dan sedikit air panas, maka ia akan berfungsi sebagai pencahar dan membersihkan tubuh dari kotoran yang berbahaya. Jahe adalah bahan yang efektif

——— Thibbun Nabawi dalam salep yang melarutkan dahak.

Jahe asam bersifat panas dan kering; ia merangsang hasrat seksual, meningkatkan produksi sperma, memanaskan lambung dan hati, membantu proses pencernaan, mengeringkan dahak yang menguasai tubuh, dan meningkatkan daya ingat. Ia juga cocok untuk mengatasi dingin pada hati dan lambung serta menghilangkan efek berbahaya dari memakan buah-buahan. Ia juga memperbaiki aroma dan menangkal bahaya makanan yang kental dan dingin.

Huruf Sin 1. Senna

Kami telah menyebutkan Senna sebelumnya dan menyatakan bahwa ia juga disebut Sanut. Terdapat tujuh pendapat mengenai apa yang dimaksud dengan kata Senna.

Senna dikatakan berarti madu, cairan kental yang muncul di permukaan mentega dan tampak seperti garis-garis hitam, biji yang menyerupai jintan, jintan Persia, dill, kurma kering, atau adas.

Safarjal bersifat dingin dan kering, menyebabkan sembelit, dan bermanfaat bagi lambung. Safarjal manis bersifat dingin dan kering serta agak lunak. Safarjal asam lebih banyak menyebabkan sembelit daripada safarjal manis dan lebih dingin serta lebih kering. Semua jenis safarjal menghilangkan dahaga, menghentikan muntah, membantu produksi urin, dan menyebabkan sembelit. Safarjal juga membantu mengatasi tukak lambung, hemoptisis (batuk darah), diare, dan mual. Safarjal juga mencegah naiknya materi gas ketika dimakan setelah makan. Selain itu, abu daun dan batang safarjal yang telah dicuci memiliki manfaat serupa dengan seng.

Mengonsumsi safarjal sebelum makan menyebabkan sembelit, sedangkan mengonsumsinya setelah makan melunakkan feses dan membantu proses pencernaan. Memakan safarjal dalam jumlah berlebihan membahayakan saraf dan menyebabkan sembelit yang menyakitkan. Safarjal membantu meredakan empedu kuning pada lambung.

Apabila safarjal dipanggang, ia akan menjadi lebih lunak dan ringan. Apabila seseorang mengeluarkan biji safarjal, mengisinya dengan madu, mencelupkannya dalam adonan, kemudian memanggangnya di bawah abu panas, maka safarjal tersebut akan menjadi sangat bermanfaat.

Cara terbaik memakan safarjal adalah dipanggang atau dimasak dalam madu. Biji safarjal juga membantu mengatasi kekeringan pada tenggorokan, saluran napas, dan beberapa penyakit lainnya. Minyaknya menghentikan keringat dan menguatkan lambung. Selai safarjal juga menguatkan lambung dan hati serta menyegarkan hati dan jiwa.

—— Thibbun Nabawi

Dalam Shahihain diriwayatkan bahwa Nabi bersabda:

"Seandainya bukan karena kekhawatiranku akan memberatkan umatku, niscaya aku akan memerintahkan mereka untuk menggunakan siwak sebelum setiap salah."

Selain itu, dalam Shahihain juga diriwayatkan bahwa:

"Setiap kali Nabi bangun di malam hari, beliau membersihkan mulutnya dengan siwak."

Al-Bukhari juga meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi bersabda:

"Siwak membersihkan mulut dan mendatangkan keridhaan Allah." Selain itu, Muslim meriwayatkan bahwa:

"Setiap kali Nabi memasuki rumahnya, beliau selalu menggunakan siwak terlebih dahulu."

Masih banyak hadith lain mengenai hal ini.

Sebuah hadith yang shahih juga menyebutkan bahwa sesaat sebelum wafat, Nabi menggunakan siwak. Nabi bersabda:

"Aku telah menyulitkan kalian dengan seringnya aku menekankan penggunaan

siwak." Jenis siwak terbaik terbuat dari pohon Arak. Selain itu, seseorang tidak boleh menggunakan pohon yang tidak dikenal untuk siwak karena mungkin saja beracun. Selain itu, siwak harus digunakan secukupnya, karena dapat menyebabkan lapisan gigi terkikis sehingga gigi menjadi rentan terhadap uap atau gas panas yang naik dari lambung serta berbagai kotoran. Apabila siwak digunakan secukupnya, ia akan memoles gigi, menguatkan akar gigi, membantu fungsi lidah, mencegah karang gigi, mewangikan napas, menjernihkan pikiran, dan menguatkan nafsu makan.

Cara terbaik menggunakan siwak adalah dengan merendamnya dalam air mawar. Selain itu, jenis siwak terbaik terbuat dari batang pohon kenari. Penulis At-Taysir berkata, "Disebutkan bahwa apabila seseorang menggunakan siwak sekali setiap lima hari, ia akan menjernihkan kepala, mempertajam indera, dan menajamkan pikiran."

Siwak bermanfaat karena banyak alasan, seperti mewangikan napas, menguatkan gusi, membersihkan dahak dan penglihatan, serta mencegah gigi berlubang. Ia juga menjaga kesehatan lambung, menjernihkan suara, membantu proses pencernaan, membantu memperjelas ucapan, dan mendorong seseorang untuk membaca Al-Quran, berdzikir kepada Allah, dan salah. Siwak juga melawan rasa kantuk, mendatangkan keridhaan Allah, menyenangkan para malaikat, dan menambah jumlah amal kebaikan.

——— Thibbun Nabawi

Siwak dianjurkan pada setiap saat, terutama sebelum salah, berwudhu, ketika bangun tidur, dan ketika mulut mengeluarkan bau tidak sedap. Ia juga dianjurkan bagi orang yang berpuasa dan semua orang pada setiap saat. Terdapat hadith-hadith yang memperbolehkan praktik ini bagi orang yang berpuasa, karena ia bermanfaat bagi mereka dan mendatangkan keridhaan Allah. Mendatangkan keridhaan Allah ketika seseorang berpuasa bahkan lebih diinginkan daripada waktu-waktu lainnya. Selain itu, siwak membersihkan mulut, dan kebersihan bagi orang yang berpuasa adalah salah satu amal terbaiknya.

Dalam Sunan diriwayatkan bahwa 'Amir bin Rabi'ah berkata: "Aku melihat Rasulullah pada banyak kesempatan menggunakan siwak saat beliau berpuasa."

Al-Bukhari juga menyebutkan bahwa Ibn 'Umar berkata bahwa Nabi menggunakan siwak di dua ujung hari.

Terdapat kesepakatan bahwa orang yang berpuasa berkumur-kumur ketika berwudhu, dan berkumur-kumur merupakan kewajiban atau tindakan yang lebih dianjurkan daripada menggunakan siwak. Selain itu, Allah tidak menginginkan manusia beribadah kepada-Nya sementara mulut mereka mengeluarkan bau tidak sedap, dan itu pun bukan merupakan ibadah. Ketika Nabi menyebutkan bahwa aroma yang dikeluarkan mulut orang yang berpuasa lebih baik di sisi-Nya daripada wangi musk, beliau bermaksud mendorong praktik berpuasa, bukan

membiarkan mulut mengeluarkan bau tidak sedap. Oleh karena itu, orang yang berpuasa lebih membutuhkan siwak daripada siapa pun. Selain itu, keridhaan Allah jauh lebih utama daripada kecintaan-Nya terhadap aroma yang dikeluarkan mulut orang yang berpuasa.

Lebih jauh lagi, Allah lebih menyukai penggunaan siwak daripada membiarkan aroma yang keluar dari mulut orang yang berpuasa tetap ada.

Selain itu, penggunaan siwak tidak mencegah aroma harum yang Allah ciptakan dalam mulut orang yang berpuasa pada Hari Kebangkitan. Bahkan, mulut orang yang berpuasa akan berbau lebih harum daripada musk pada Hari Kebangkitan sebagai tanda ketaatannya menjalankan puasa, meskipun siwak telah menghilangkan aroma yang sebelumnya keluar dari mulutnya. Demikian pula, barangsiapa yang menderita luka [di jalan Allah] di dunia ini diperintahkan untuk membersihkan darah dari tubuhnya di dunia ini, namun lukanya tetap akan berwarna darah pada Hari Kebangkitan.

Selain itu, Siwak tidak sepenuhnya menghilangkan bau mulut orang yang berpuasa karena penyebabnya masih ada, yaitu perut yang kosong dari makanan. Hanya saja, efek dari kondisi ini akan berkurang pada gigi dan gusi.

Nabi ﷺ mengajarkan kepada umatnya tentang amalan-amalan yang disukai dan yang tidak disukai dalam puasa. Beliau tidak menyebutkan Siwak

—— Thibbun Nabawi

di antara amalan-amalan yang tidak disukai, meskipun beliau mengetahui bahwa mereka menggunakannya. Bahkan, beliau menganjurkan mereka secara umum untuk menggunakan Siwak dan mereka melihat beliau menggunakannya sendiri dalam kesempatan yang tak terhitung, dengan mengetahui bahwa mereka akan meneladaninya. Beliau tidak pernah melarang mereka menggunakan Siwak di sore hari (ketika mereka sedang berpuasa). Merupakan suatu kenyataan bahwa Nabi ﷺ berkewajiban menyampaikan hukum agama ketika diperlukan.

Terdapat sebuah hadith yang lemah yang meriwayatkan bahwa Nabi ﷺ menganjurkan kaum Muslimin untuk meminum susu sapi, karena ia adalah obat dan karena mentega dari susu tersebut adalah penyembuh, sambil menyatakan bahwa daging sapi adalah penyakit. Sanad perawi hadith ini tidak sahih.

Samn bersifat panas dan basah pada derajat pertama, sifatnya lembut, dan membantu menghilangkan berbagai tumor yang menyerang bagian-bagian tubuh yang lunak. Samn lebih kuat daripada mentega sebagai agen pematang. Galinus berkata bahwa ia menggunakan lemak untuk mengobati tumor yang terjadi di telinga dan ujung hidung. Selain itu, apabila pangkal gigi diolesi dengan lemak, gigi akan tumbuh lebih cepat.

"Whenever النبي ﷺ b would enter his house, he would first use the Siwak."

There are many other Ahadith on this subject.

An authentic الحديث In addition stated that just before he died, النبي ﷺ b used the Siwak. النبي ﷺ b said:

I have troubled you by frequently stressing using

the Siwak." The best type of Siwak is made from Arak trees. Further, one should not use an unknown tree for Siwak for it might be poisonous. In addition, Siwak should be used moderately, because it might cause the coating of the teeth to wear away and thus the teeth will be susceptible to the hot vapors or gasses that ascend from the stomach and to all kinds of dirt. When the Siwak is used moderately, it will polish the teeth, strengthen the roots of the teeth, help the tongue, prevent plaque, perfume the breath, clear the mind and strengthen the appetite.

The best way to use the Swak is by soaking it in rose water. In addition, the best type of Siwak is made from walnut stems. The author of At-Taysir said, "It was said that when one uses the Siwak once every five days, it will clear the head, sharpen the senses and alert the mind.”

Siwak is beneficial for many reasons, such as perfuming the breath, strengthening the gingiva, clearing the phlegm and the sight and preventing cavities. It In addition preserves the health of the stomach, clears the voice, helps the digestion process, helps make the speech clear and encourages one to recite the Qura'an, remember الله and pray. Siwak In addition fights sleepiness, pleases the Lord, delights the angels and increases the number of the good deeds.

——— The Prophetic Medicine

Siwak is preferred at any time, especially before the prayer, ablution, when waking up and when the mouth emits foul odor. It is In addition preferred for those who are fasting and all other people at all times. There are Ahadith that allow this practice for those who are fasting, because it is beneficial for them and pleases the Lord. Pleasing the Lord when one is fasting is even more desired than at other times. In addition the Siwak cleans the mouth, and cleanliness for the fasting person is one of his best deeds.

In the Sunan it is narrated that ‘Amir bin RabT'ah said: "I saw رسول الله ﷺ b on nvmerous occasion using the Siwak when he was fasting."

AlI-Bukhari In addition said that Ibn 'Umar said that النبي ﷺ sg used the Siwak at the two ends of the day.

There is a consensus that the fasting person rinses his mouth while performing ablution, and rinsing the mouth is an obligation or a more preferred act than using the Siwak. In addition, الله does not have a use for the people to worship him while their mouths are emitting foul odor, nor is it an act of worship. When النبي ﷺ mentioned that the odor that the mouth of the fasting person emits is better in His Sight than the scent of Musk, he meant to encourage the practice of fasting, not the

practice of allowing the mouth to emit foul odor. Hence, the fasting person needs the Siwak more than anyone else. In addition, الله's Pleasure is much better than His preferring the odor that the mouth of the fasting person emits.

Further, الله prefers using the Siwak more than for the odor that comes out of the mouth of the fasting person to remain.

In addition, using Siwak does not prevent the good scent that الله creates in the fasting person's mouth on the Day of Resurrection. Rather, the mouth of the fasting person will smell better than Musk on the Day of Resurrection as a sign of his observing the Fast, even if the Siwak removed the odor that used to come from his mouth. Similarly, whoever suffers a wound [for the sake of الله] in this life is commanded to remove the blood from his body in this life, but his wound would still have the color of blood on the Day of Resurrection.

Furthermore, the Siwak does not completely remove the odor of the mouth for the fasting person because its reason still remains, that is, the stomach being empty of food. Only the effect of this fact will be lessened from the teeth and gums.

النبي ﷺ b taught his nation the preferred and the disliked acts of the Fast. He did not mention the Siwak

—— The Prophetic Medicine

among the disliked acts although he knew they were using it. Rather, he encouraged them in general to use the Siwak and they saw him using it himself at innumerable times, knowing that they would imitate him. Never did he discourage them from using the Siwak in the afternoon (when they are fasting). It is a fact that النبي ﷺ “g is required to offer the religious ruling when it is needed.

There is a weak الحديث that narrates that النبي ﷺ as encouraged the Muslim to drink cow milk, because it is a remedy and because the butter of that milk is a cure, while stating that cow meat is an ailment, The chain of narrators for this الحديث is not authentic.

Somn is hot and wet in the first degree, it is mild, and helps eliminate the various tumors that attack the soft parts of the body. Somn is stronger than butter as a maturing agent, Galinus said that he used shortening to cure the tumors that occur in the ear and the tip of the nose. In addition, when the base of the teeth is anointed with shortening, the teeth will emerge faster.
